r4clinstats 0.3.0

Get started

Source: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd
getting-started.Rmd

This guide takes you from a blank computer to your first lesson. It assumes no prior experience with R. Set aside about fifteen minutes the first time.

1. Install R and RStudio

Both are free.

  • R is the engine. Download it from CRAN and install it like any other program.
  • RStudio is the comfortable workspace you run R in. Download RStudio Desktop and install that too.

Open RStudio once both are in place. You will see a panel called the Console, with a > prompt. That is where the commands below go: type or paste a line, then press Enter.

2. Install the course

The course is an R package called r4clinstats. It is not on CRAN, so you install it from GitHub. Paste these two lines into the Console:

install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("apsmunro/r4clinstats")

The first line fetches a small helper. The second downloads the course and the packages it needs. This can take a few minutes the first time, and some red progress text along the way is normal.

3. Check your setup

Load the package and run the built-in check:

library(r4clinstats)
check_setup()

check_setup() looks over your machine, confirms the pieces are in place, and offers to install anything missing. That includes gradethis, which gives you the feedback in each exercise and comes from Posit’s R-universe rather than CRAN. Say yes to any prompts.

4. Start learning 

learn()        # list the lessons
learn("m0")    # open the first one

learn() shows the menu. learn("m0") opens a lesson in your browser, where you read a little, run real code in the boxes, and get feedback as you go. Start at m0 and work along in order.

That is everything. The rest you learn by doing.

If something goes wrong

A few things trip people up. None of them are your fault.

“No tutorials found for package ‘r4clinstats’.” Your R session is holding an older view of the package. In RStudio, choose Session ▸ Restart R, then run library(r4clinstats) and try learn("m0") again.

Install fails on a work or managed computer. Some workplaces store your documents in OneDrive, or lock down where programs may write, which R does not get along with. Run this once:

library(r4clinstats)
use_local_library()

It sets up a private folder for your R packages in a location that always works, then asks you to restart R. After restarting, repeat step 2.

A package will not install from CRAN. Behind some hospital and university firewalls, check_setup() reports what it cannot reach and names the fix, usually pointing R at Posit’s package mirror.

More problems, and their fixes, are on the Troubleshooting page, written for hospital and university computers. Still stuck? Open an issue at the project page and describe what you saw.

On this page

Developed by A. P. S. Munro.
